slides.text: Today I visit the dentist with a trusted grown-up. Dentists keep teeth healthy, and we go to see what will happen next.
slides.image_prompt: child walking into a friendly dental office with caregiver, calm colors, storybook style
slides.parent_tip: Preview the schedule card and remind the child that the visit keeps teeth strong.

slides.text: The waiting room may smell minty and sound quiet. I can sit on a comfy chair and hold my fidget while I wait.
slides.image_prompt: child sitting in dentist waiting room holding small fidget toy, gentle lighting
slides.parent_tip: Offer noise-reducing headphones or a fidget if the room is busy.

slides.text: When the hygienist says my name, I walk with them to the big chair. Grown-ups stay nearby and smile at me.
slides.image_prompt: dental hygienist greeting child, walking toward dental chair, encouraging expression
slides.parent_tip: Use a simple gesture cue (thumbs up) so the child knows you are close.

slides.text: The light may feel bright, so I can wear cool sunglasses. If the tools hum, I breathe in for four counts and out for four counts.
slides.image_prompt: child wearing sunglasses in dental chair, dentist nearby with small mirror
slides.parent_tip: Model the breathing script together before tools begin.

slides.text: When the dentist checks my teeth, I keep my hands on my lap and stay as still as I can. The exam is short and helps my smile.
slides.image_prompt: dentist calmly examining child's teeth, child relaxed with hands on lap
slides.parent_tip: Offer a silent countdown using fingers so the child knows how much time is left.

slides.text: After we finish, I rinse, sit up slowly, and choose a sticker. I did hard work and can feel proud of taking care of my teeth.
slides.image_prompt: child receiving sticker from dental assistant, smiling proudly
slides.parent_tip: Celebrate effort over perfection—name one strategy the child used well.

slides.text: Haircut day keeps my hair neat so I can see and play. A stylist trims a little bit at a time.
slides.image_prompt: child entering salon with caregiver, stylist waving hello
slides.parent_tip: Show a before/after picture or mirror selfie to anchor the goal.

slides.text: The salon may have hair dryers, mirrors, and soft music. I can wear a comfy cape or ask for a towel instead.
slides.image_prompt: child sitting in salon chair with cape, observing mirrors and lights
slides.parent_tip: Pack a preferred cape or t-shirt clip if textures bother the child.

slides.text: When it is my turn, I climb into the chair and place my feet on the bar. I can hold a small fidget in one hand.
slides.image_prompt: child sitting in salon chair gripping small fidget, stylist preparing tools
slides.parent_tip: Offer a weighted lap pad or gentle hand squeeze for grounding.

slides.text: Scissors make quiet snips near my ears. If I feel tickly, I take a deep breath and say, 'Please brush the hair off, thank you.'
slides.image_prompt: stylist trimming child's hair, child practicing calm breathing
slides.parent_tip: Coach the polite script beforehand and echo it calmly if needed.

slides.text: Loose hair might fall on my neck. I can ask for a soft brush or towel so it feels better while we finish.
slides.image_prompt: child smiling while stylist brushes hair off neck with towel
slides.parent_tip: Pack hypoallergenic wipes or a damp cloth for quick cleanup.

slides.text: When the haircut is done, I thank the stylist and look in the mirror. I did it! Clean hair helps me feel fresh and ready.
slides.image_prompt: child admiring new haircut in mirror, caregiver smiling
slides.parent_tip: Name one coping tool the child used so it sticks for next time.

slides.text: Sometimes my school practices a fire drill. It is a safety rehearsal so everyone knows where to go.
slides.image_prompt: students lined up in classroom preparing for drill, teacher explaining calmly
slides.parent_tip: Review the school map or photo of the meeting spot before the day starts.

slides.text: The alarm is loud like a beeping robot. I can cover my ears or wear headphones while we walk.
slides.image_prompt: child covering ears gently while alarm lights flash, teacher guiding
slides.parent_tip: Coordinate with school so headphones are stored near the desk.

slides.text: When the alarm beeps, I stand up, line up behind the line leader, and keep my hands by my sides.
slides.image_prompt: students forming line, child following visual footstep markers
slides.parent_tip: Practice a short lining-up routine at home with a timer for muscle memory.

slides.text: Hallways might feel busy. I look at the person in front of me and take bunny breaths (short nose breaths) until we reach outside.
slides.image_prompt: child in hallway focusing on peer ahead, taking calm breaths
slides.parent_tip: Teach bunny breaths with a visual card clipped to the backpack.

slides.text: Outside, we stand on our class spot while teachers count. I stay quiet so they hear names.
slides.image_prompt: class lined up outside school, teacher holding clipboard
slides.parent_tip: Celebrate with a small movement break when the child returns indoors.

slides.text: When the drill ends, the alarm stops and we walk back calmly. Practicing helps keep everyone safe, and I can feel proud of following the plan.
slides.image_prompt: students walking back inside calmly, child smiling
slides.parent_tip: Reflect on one success (“You covered your ears and stayed in line”) to reinforce confidence.
